screen: hero-passwords (Hero variant A — Dark Web Exposure Assessment)
exportDimensions: Export read at 3582×1352 (displayed 2000×755). It is a ~2x asset, so CSS px ≈ original/2. Full-bleed hero band is ~3582×1352 native ≈ 1791×676 CSS. This is a desktop hero section (likely one row of a taller page), a 2-column split: left text column ~ native x=760→1860 (CSS ~380→930, col width ~550 CSS), right mockup card ~ native x=1970→3400 (CSS ~985→1700, card width ~525 CSS). Global horizontal padding to first content ~ CSS 210px left / centered max-width container ~1290px.
layoutSummary: Full-width dark hero with a radial indigo glow in the top-right. Two-column layout inside a centered max-width container (~1290px). LEFT column (≈50% width): eyebrow label, huge 3-line extrabold headline with a green mid-phrase and colored punctuation, supporting paragraph, an inline email-capture form (pill input + green CTA in one bordered rounded container), a shield reassurance line, and a bulleted fine-print trust row. RIGHT column (≈50%): a dark macOS-style 'terminal' scan-result card containing a domain header row with a COMPROMISED pill, a 3-up stat-tile grid, an 'exposure timeline' line-chart card, a leaked-credential result row with a 'meta' pill, and an italic caption. Vertical rhythm is generous; left column is vertically centered against the taller card on the right.

sections.name: Page background / ambient glow
sections.description: Full-bleed very dark indigo/navy base with a soft radial purple glow bleeding in from the top-right corner behind the terminal card, giving depth. No visible texture or noise; smooth gradient falloff toward near-black at bottom-left.
sections.notes: Glow center sits roughly behind/above the card top-right. Radial gradient from ~#3a2a9a fading to #0E0A2C. Bottom-left corner is the darkest. Keep it subtle — it never washes out text contrast.

sections.name: Eyebrow / kicker label
sections.description: Uppercase tracked micro-label 'DARK WEB EXPOSURE ASSESSMENT FOR BUSINESS' above the headline, in violet.
sections.notes: ~13px CSS, weight 700, letter-spacing ~0.12em, all-caps. Sits ~40px above headline.

sections.name: Headline
sections.description: Three-line extrabold headline: 'Your employees'' passwords are already on the dark web. You just haven't looked.' Mixed color: most words near-white; 'already on the dark web' in emerald green; the period after 'web' is red; final 'You just haven't looked.' returns to white with a red/green-tinted terminal period.
sections.notes: ~58–64px CSS, weight 800, line-height ~1.02–1.05, letter-spacing slightly tight (~-0.01em). The green span and the accent-colored full stops are the key detail. Apostrophe in 'employees'' is a straight typographic quote.

sections.name: Supporting paragraph (subhead body)
sections.description: Two-line paragraph: 'One scan shows every company login already leaked on the dark web — and exactly which systems they open.'
sections.notes: ~20px CSS, weight 500, line-height ~1.5. Em-dash used. Sits ~28px below headline.

sections.name: Email capture form (inline)
sections.description: A single rounded, subtly bordered container holding a borderless email input ('Enter your business email' placeholder) on the left and a solid green pill CTA 'Scan Company Domain →' on the right.
sections.layout: horizontal: input (flex-grow) + button (auto), inside one bordered rounded shell
sections.notes: Shell radius ~14px, faint 1px border. Inner CTA radius ~10px, sits with ~6px inset padding from shell. Input placeholder ~18px weight 500. Button label ~18px weight 700, dark ink on green, trailing → arrow. ~40px below paragraph.

sections.name: Reassurance line (shield)
sections.description: Small green shield icon + two-line text: 'No software to install and no network access required. We check your domain against dark web breach data externally — nothing ever touches your systems.'
sections.notes: ~16px CSS, weight 500, line-height ~1.45. Icon ~16px, top-aligned to first line. ~24px below form.

sections.name: Trust bullet row (fine print)
sections.description: Single line of dot-separated micro-benefits: 'Free for a limited time • No credit card • Your email is only used to send the report'.
sections.notes: ~14px CSS, weight 500. Middle-dot • separators with spacing. ~18px below shield line.

sections.name: Terminal / scan-result card (right)
sections.description: Dark rounded card styled as a macOS terminal window. Title bar with 3 traffic-light dots and 'exposure_scan — yourcompany.com' in mono; body contains the domain header, stat tiles, chart, result row, and caption.
sections.layout: vertical stack inside a rounded bordered card
sections.notes: Card radius ~16–18px, 1px subtle border ~rgba(255,255,255,0.08), soft outer shadow + the ambient glow behind it. Title bar has bottom hairline divider. Dots: red #FF5C5C, amber #FFC668, green #7DE3B0, ~11px each. Card inner padding ~24px CSS.

sections.name: Domain header row (inside card)
sections.description: '> yourcompany.com' in mono white on the left; a red 'COMPROMISED' status pill with a leading red dot on the right.
sections.notes: Pill radius full (~999px), ~1px red border, tinted translucent red fill, uppercase ~12px mono/700, leading 6px dot.

sections.name: Stat-tile grid
sections.description: Three equal tiles: '266 / Corporate identities leaked' (red), '105 / Systems & apps exposed' (orange), '10 / Malware families' (blue).
sections.notes: Big number ~34px CSS weight 800; label ~13px weight 500 gray, up to 2 lines. Tile radius ~12px, subtle border, inner padding ~16px.

sections.name: Exposure timeline chart card
sections.description: Nested darker card labeled 'exposure timeline' (mono, top-left) with '2020 → 2026' range (mono, top-right), containing a red line chart with a faint red area fill, trending upward.
sections.layout: card with header row + chart area below
keyColors: area fill red fade #39...1F → transparent / rgba(255,92,92,0.12)
sections.notes: Radius ~12px. Chart ~7–8 segment jagged upward line, no visible axes/gridlines, single series. Area fill fades to transparent at bottom.

sections.name: Credential result row
sections.description: One mono result line: 'c**********2@yourcompany.com' (masked, cyan) '> login.microsoftonline.com' (white) with a small red 'meta' pill at far right.
sections.layout: row: masked email + chevron + system, then meta pill right-aligned
keyColors: meta pill text #FF5C5C on tinted red bg #432121
sections.notes: Mono ~14px. 'meta' pill small radius ~6px, tinted red fill, ~11px mono.

sections.name: Card caption
sections.description: Centered italic disclaimer: 'Figures shown are from an anonymized real scan.'
sections.notes: ~13px CSS, italic, weight 400. ~16px below result row, inside card bottom padding.

typography.notes: dark ink on green, trailing arrow →
MONOSPACE typeface (e.g. SF Mono / JetBrains Mono), NOT Plus Jakarta Sans — the whole card uses mono
uppercase for COMPROMISED, lowercase mono for meta

reusableComponents.name: PrimaryButton
reusableComponents.description: Solid emerald-green pill CTA with dark ink and optional trailing arrow icon
reusableComponents.variants: sizes: md (form inset). states: default/hover(darken ~8%)/focus/disabled. with-icon | text-only

reusableComponents.name: EmailCaptureForm
reusableComponents.description: Bordered rounded shell containing a borderless input + inline PrimaryButton
reusableComponents.variants: layout: inline (desktop) | stacked (mobile). states: default/focused(border brighten)/error

reusableComponents.name: StatTile
reusableComponents.description: Bordered dark tile: big colored number + gray multiline label
reusableComponents.variants: accent: red | orange | blue | green. label 1-line | 2-line

reusableComponents.name: StatusPill
reusableComponents.description: Rounded translucent-tinted pill with optional leading dot
reusableComponents.variants: tone: red/COMPROMISED (dot) | small tag 'meta'. shape: full-round | soft (r6). with-dot | no-dot

reusableComponents.name: TerminalCard
reusableComponents.description: macOS-window-style dark card: title bar w/ 3 traffic dots + mono title, divider, body slot

reusableComponents.name: MiniLineChartCard
reusableComponents.description: Nested inset card with mono title + range label and a single-series area line chart

reusableComponents.name: CredentialRow
reusableComponents.description: Mono result row: masked email + chevron + target system + tag pill

charts: Exposure timeline: single-series area LINE chart, jagged upward trend across ~7-8 points (2020→2026). Line stroke #FF5C5C ~2px; area fill a vertical red gradient rgba(255,92,92,~0.15) fading to transparent (palette match #39D98A1F pattern but red-tinted / #432121 low-alpha). No axes, ticks, or gridlines rendered — minimal decorative sparkline style on dark #0B0E14 ground.

assetsNeeded.name: shield-check-icon
assetsNeeded.note: small green shield in reassurance line, ~16px

assetsNeeded.name: chevron-right-icon
assetsNeeded.note: '>' before domain and before target system in mono rows

assetsNeeded.name: traffic-light-dots
assetsNeeded.note: 3 window dots (can be pure CSS circles)

assetsNeeded.name: radial-glow-bg
assetsNeeded.note: top-right indigo/violet radial glow — CSS radial-gradient, no image needed

assetsNeeded.name: monospace-font
assetsNeeded.note: terminal card requires a monospace family (e.g. JetBrains Mono / SF Mono) in addition to Plus Jakarta Sans for the marketing text

fidelityNotes: Ambient glow: top-right radial-gradient from ~#3a2a9a/#5B3DF5 fading into base #0E0A2C, darkest at bottom-left (#0B0E14). Must sit BEHIND the card and never reduce text contrast.

Headline is multi-color inline: white default, emerald green span on 'already on the dark web', and RED full-stops (the '.' after web and the terminal-style '.' accents) — these tiny colored periods are easy to miss.

Green tone: CTA + green headline span read as a vivid emerald (~#2ECB8C); palette's closest are #7DE3B0 (lighter mint) and #00BA00 (too pure). Tune between these; CTA slightly more saturated than the headline mint.

EmailCaptureForm is a nested-border pattern: outer faint 1px lavender border (rgba ~#D9D9D933) radius ~14px, inner green button radius ~10px with ~6px inset — the button does NOT touch the shell edges.
Card border is a hairline ~1px rgba(255,255,255,0.06-0.08); title bar has its own bottom divider hairline. Card has a soft drop shadow plus the glow.
Pills use translucent tinted fills over dark (COMPROMISED: red fill ~rgba red low-alpha + ~1px red border + leading dot; meta: solid-ish tinted red #432121 bg, small radius).
Stat tiles: subtle border + very slightly lighter fill than card; big numbers are extrabold and each a different accent hue.
Chart area gradient fades to transparent at the baseline; line has no data-point markers.
Two font families in play — sans (marketing) vs mono (entire card). Don't render the card text in Plus Jakarta.
Border radii ladder: card ~16-18px, tiles/chart-card ~12px, form shell ~14px, button ~10px, pills full/6px.
Muted lavender text hierarchy: body #CFC1D8 > reassurance #A989C8 > fine print #897398 — three distinct dim-violet steps, not plain gray.
interactions: Email input: focus brightens shell border; validation error state (red border) on invalid business email.
Scan Company Domain button: hover darkens green ~8% and/or nudges the → arrow right; active press; loading/scanning spinner state after submit.
Terminal card is illustrative/static, but data rows (credential row, stat tiles) could animate in on scroll (count-up numbers, line-draw for the chart).
COMPROMISED pill dot could pulse/blink to imply live status.
Chart could reveal via left-to-right stroke-dashoffset animation on view.
Trailing arrow and chevrons are decorative (no per-item interaction).
Masked email implies a copy-to-reveal or reassurance-of-privacy affordance, though none shown as active here.
responsiveNotes: Desktop (shown): 2-column, text left / card right, container max-width ~1290px, ~50/50 split with generous gutter. Tablet (~768-1024px): stack to single column — headline + form on top, terminal card below full-width; reduce headline to ~40-44px; stat tiles remain 3-up. Mobile (<640px): single column, container padding ~20-24px; headline ~30-34px, 800 weight, keep the green span; supporting paragraph full-width; EmailCaptureForm switches to STACKED (full-width input above full-width button, both radius ~12px); reassurance + bullet row wrap naturally (bullets may become a 2-line list); terminal card full-width, card inner padding ~16px, stat tiles collapse from 3-up to a 3-across compact or 1-column stack, mono font-size ~12px, chart height reduces; caption stays centered. Ambient glow should reposition to top-center/behind card on mobile. Maintain min contrast for the dim-violet text tiers at all sizes.
